topics: 🐎 The wagging tail (כִּישְׁכְּשָׁה בִּזְנָבָהּ) — full vs. half damage
🛢️ Stepping on a skin of oil: container פָּטוּר full, contents חֲצִי נֶזֶק
🪞 Two stacked tables — upper full, lower half damage
🏃 Stepping in רה"ר that ricochets into רה"י: stepping or breaking?
🐔 Mishnah opens: chickens are מוּעָדִין לְהַלֵּךְ כְּדַרְכָּן וּלְשַׁבֵּר

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Where רגל Is Full and Where It Is Only Half
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf opens mid-conversation between R' Hoshaia the Elder and R' Yudan Nesia. R' Abba bar Mamal walks in and asks about a mule that wags its tail and breaks something — is the owner liable in full, or only half? The daf then runs through a series of cases where one act of regel-style movement causes layered damage: a skin of oil, glass on a table, two stacked tables, and a ricochet from public into private property. The cases all probe one question: when normal walking damage breaks something the animal does not normally break, where does חֲצִי נֶזֶק begin?
overview.hebrewSource: וּמַה פְשִׁיטָא לוֹן. בְּנֶזֶק שָׁלֵם. וּמַה צְרִיכָה לוֹן. בַּחֲצִי נֶזֶק

slides.title: 💬 The Mule That Wagged Its Tail
slides.subtitle: R' Abba bar Mamal walks into the beit midrash with a question.

lines.speaker: R' Abba
lines.text: כִּישְׁכְּשָׁה בִּזְנָבָהּ. כְּגוֹן אִילֵּין פֵּירְדָתָא. מָהוּ? If a mule wags its tail and breaks something — what is the law?

lines.speaker: Silence
lines.text: וְלָא אָמְרִין לֵיהּ כְּלוּם — at first they said nothing.

lines.speaker: R' Hoshaia
lines.text: If not, what would we say? צָרִיךְ שֶׁיְּהֵא תוֹפֵשׂ בִּזְנָבָהּ — must the owner be holding its tail? Only when the tail-wagging is unusual. If it is normal — yes, the owner must literally hold the tail.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: Bottom line: פְשִׁיטָא לוֹן בְּנֶזֶק שָׁלֵם — full damages were obvious. צְרִיכָה לוֹן בַּחֲצִי נֶזֶק — what they were unsure about was the half-damage case.

callout.label: Key move:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi distinguishes between the animal's regular behavior (full damage, normal regel) and its unusual behavior (half damage, like keren). The mule's wagging tail forces a fresh ruling at the seam between the two.

slides.title: 📊 Layered Damage Cases (R' Yirmiyah's Series)
slides.subtitle: When one stomp breaks two things, each layer is judged on its own.

callout.label: Pattern:
callout.text: What the foot directly tramples is normal regel — full damage. What gets broken indirectly, by the trampled object collapsing, is treated like keren-style abnormal damage — only half.

slides.title: 🏃 The Ricochet Case — רה"ר → רה"י
slides.subtitle: R' Yirmiyah's hardest case: where does liability attach?

nodes.content:
טַבֶּלָּה מוּנַחַת בִּרְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים
Animal steps on it in the public domain

הִתִּיזָה וְהָלְכָה וְנִשְׁתַּבְּרָה בִרְשׁוּת הַיָּחִיד
The piece flies off and shatters in private property

columns.label: Follow the stepping
columns.content:
אַחַר דְּרִיסָתָהּ — פָּטוּר
Stepping was in רה"ר → exempt

columns.label: Follow the breaking
columns.content:
אַחַר שְׁבִירָתָהּ — חַיָיב
Breaking was in רה"י → liable

nodes.content: The Yerushalmi leaves the question framed as a clean fork: do we trace liability to the act of pressure or to the moment of breakage?

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: Domain (רה"ר vs. רה"י) is decisive for רגל liability. The ricochet case forces us to identify exactly when the damage 'happened' — a question that returns later in the masechet.

slides.title: 🐔 Entering Chapter 2:2 — The Mishnah of Chickens
slides.subtitle: The new Mishnah at the end of the daf sets up the next sugya.

boxes.title: 🐔 Normal walking
items: Chickens are 'notorious' for breaking things just by walking
Implies full damage for ordinary stride breakage

boxes.title: 🪢 Tied thread on foot
items: A string trailing from the foot snags vessels

callout.label: Setup for daf 9:
callout.text: The Mishnah seeds the next debate: when is a tied thread the owner's fault (he tied it) versus an act-of-nature (it tied itself)? Daf 9 opens with Rav Huna's distinction.

slides.title: 🧠 The Underlying Principle
slides.subtitle: How the Yerushalmi reads the chain of cases on this daf.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Identify whether the damage is the animal's normal way (דַּרְכָּהּ) or unusual (אֵין דַּרְכָּהּ).
2️⃣ Normal way → רגל or שן territory → נֶזֶק שָׁלֵם in the victim's domain.
3️⃣ Unusual / indirect → keren-style damage → חֲצִי נֶזֶק.
4️⃣ When one act produces both — like a stomp + a glass shatter — split the payment per layer.
5️⃣ Domain matters: even full-damage cases can collapse if the act started in רה"ר.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The daf's whole engine is the seam between regel and keren. Every case asks: which side of that line are we on, and where in space and time did the damage occur?

summary.principle: When normal walking causes layered damage, peel the case apart: direct contact is full damage, indirect breakage is half. Domain and timing then decide whether liability attaches at all.

flowSteps.content: Test: is this normal behavior (דַּרְכָּהּ) or not?

columns.content:
🛢️ נוֹד שֶׁמֶן
full for skin, half for oil

🪞 טבלה + זכוכית
full for table, half for glass

flowSteps.content:
🎓 Key Principle:
Direct contact in normal manner = full damage. Indirect / unusual = half. The seam between רגל and קרן structures the entire daf.

quiz.q: What case did R' Abba bar Mamal bring before R' Hoshaia and R' Yudan Nesia?
o: An ox that gored another ox
A mule that wagged its tail and broke something (כִּישְׁכְּשָׁה בִּזְנָבָהּ)
A chicken that pecked dough
A dog that jumped from a roof
quiz.e: The Yerushalmi opens: כִּישְׁכְּשָׁה בִּזְנָבָהּ. כְּגוֹן אִילֵּין פֵּירְדָתָא. מָהוּ?

quiz.q: According to R' Hoshaia, when must the owner literally grab the animal's tail?
o: Always
Never — only watch from a distance
Only when the tail-wagging is the animal's normal behavior (דַּרְכָּהּ לָכֵן)
Only on Shabbat
quiz.e: R' Hoshaia: if it is unusual behavior, no need to grab; but בְּשֶׁדַּרְכָּהּ לָכֵן צָרִיךְ שֶׁיְּהֵא תוֹפֵשׂ בִּזְנָבָהּ.

quiz.q: If an animal stepped on a skin (נוד) full of oil, what does R' Yirmiyah rule?
o: Full damages for both
Half for both
Full for the skin, half for the oil
Half for the skin, full for the oil
quiz.e: עַל הַנּוֹד מְשַׁלֵּם נֶזֶק שָׁלֵם וְעַל הַשֶּׁמֶן מְשַׁלֵּם חֲצִי נֶזֶק — direct contact pays full, indirect breakage pays half.

quiz.q: In the ricochet case (table in רה"ר → glass shatters in רה"י), what is the Yerushalmi's question?
o: Whether to follow the stepping (דְּרִיסָתָהּ) or the breaking (שְׁבִירָתָהּ)
Whether tooth or foot is more lenient
Whether the owner was negligent
Whether the animal was tame or notorious
quiz.e: אַחַר מִי אַתְּ מְהַלֵּךְ אַחַר דְּרִיסָתָהּ אוֹ אַחַר שְׁבִירָתָהּ? If we follow stepping → exempt; if breaking → liable.

quiz.q: What is the new Mishnah at the end of daf 8 (2:2)?
o: Four avot of damages
הַתַּרְנוֹגַלִּין מוּעָדִין לְהַלֵּךְ כְּדַרְכָּן וּלְשַׁבֵּר
אָדָם מוּעָד לְעוֹלָם
Tame vs. notorious bull
quiz.e: The daf ends by introducing the chicken Mishnah, which sets up the next sugya about tied threads (דְלִיל קָשׁוּר) and scraping motion.
